heading: Punishment for violation of order
label: 195 Punishment for violation of order
notes: Amendments2002— substituted “packer or swine contractor” for “packer” in two places in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 107–171
Change of NameAct , as amended by act , substituted “court of appeals” for “circuit court of appeals”, wherever appearing in this section.June 25, 1948May 24, 1949
amendments: Amendments2002— substituted “packer or swine contractor” for “packer” in two places in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 107–171
Change of NameAct , as amended by act , substituted “court of appeals” for “circuit court of appeals”, wherever appearing in this section.June 25, 1948May 24, 1949

children.text: After the expiration of the time allowed for filing a petition in the court of appeals to set aside or modify such order, if no such petition has been filed within such time; or

After the expiration of the time allowed for applying for a writ of certiorari, if such order, or such order as modified, has been sustained by the court of appeals and no such writ has been applied for within such time; or

After such order, or such order as modified, has been sustained by the courts as provided in ; shall on conviction be fined not less than $500 nor more than $10,000, or imprisoned for not less than six months nor more than five years, or both. Each day during which such failure continues shall be deemed a separate offense.section 194 of this title
